key_insight: Entropy engineering via multicomponent B-site substitution in BF-BT creates a more pseudocubic, relaxed ferroelectric ceramic with lower hysteresis and much higher breakdown strength.
engineering_relevance: This provides a lead-free route to high-performance dielectric capacitors for pulsed power applications with improved recoverable energy density.
method: Introduce high-entropy perovskite BaTi0.2Zr0.2Sn0.2Hf0.2Nb0.1Sc0.1O3 into the BF-BT matrix
Use multicomponent B-site substitution to induce lattice distortion, grain refinement, and relaxor behavior
Suppress polarization hysteresis and conduction while promoting more uniform electric-field distribution
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a maximum breakdown strength of 840 kV/cm and a recoverable energy density of 10.55 J/cm3.
watch_item: Performance gains may depend on precise composition control and microstructural uniformity, which can be challenging to reproduce at scale.
paper_title: Entropy engineering of BF-BT-based high-entropy ceramics for ultra-high energy storage performance
